input.background: Exercise 14.1 introduced data for a cross-sectional survey of bicycle helmet use in Northern California. Table 14.5 lists the data. Exercise 14.1(a) revealed that observation 13 (Los Arboles) was an outlier.
input.data:
\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 14.5 Data for Exercise 14.1. Percent of school children receiving free or reduced-fee lunches at school (variable P_RFM) and percent of bicycle riders wearing a helmet (variable P_HELM). Data for this study was recorded by field observers in October of 1994.}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline i & School & P_RFM & P_HELM \
\hline 1 & Fair Oaks & 50 & 22.1 \
\hline 2 & Strandwood & 11 & 35.9 \
\hline 3 & Walnut Acres & 2 & 57.9 \
\hline 4 & Discov. Bay & 19 & 22.2 \
\hline 5 & Belshaw & 26 & 42.4 \
\hline 6 & Kennedy & 73 & 5.8 \
\hline 7 & Cassell & 81 & 3.6 \
\hline 8 & Miner & 51 & 21.4 \
\hline 9 & Sedgewick & 11 & 55.2 \
\hline 10 & Sakamoto & 2 & 33.3 \
\hline 11 & Toyon & 19 & 32.4 \
\hline 12 & Lietz & 25 & 38.4 \
\hline 13 & Los Arboles & 84 & 46.6 \
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
input.question: Use the 95% confidence interval to predict whether the slope is significant at α=0.05.

output.answer: Not provided in the original answer.
